description: httpx is a modern HTTP client for Python with asynchronous support and HTTP/2 + HTTP/3 support.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/httpx && python3 httpx.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/httpx && python3 httpx.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/httpx && python3 httpx.py --help`. Async HTTP client (HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the web category. Fits when the engagement needs: Async HTTP client (HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Async HTTP client (HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, HTTP/3).

name: httpx

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
